title: Master Organizational Constitution & Operating Charter v1.0
subtitle: The Founding Constitution
purpose: Permanent constitutional framework — everything in the institution flows from this document.
founders_principle: Not built merely to explain one Supreme Court decision. Built to strengthen Arkansas civic culture — every article, lesson, volunteer, leader, partner, county, neighborhood, conversation, generation.
institutional_creed: An informed citizenry is essential to self-government
Ordinary Arkansans can become extraordinary civic educators
Knowledge shared freely is one of the greatest public services

items.title: Name
items.text: Citizens United Facts — educate about Citizens United v. FEC; does not support or oppose by name

items.title: Mission
items.text: Most trusted, transparent, evidence-based educational institution for Citizens United, campaign finance, constitutional government, civic participation, and related policy

items.title: Vision
items.text: 75 counties · 250 cities · 200,000 connected · lasting statewide civic education infrastructure

items.title: Core Values
items.text: Truth, Transparency, Curiosity, Respect, Service, Stewardship

items.title: Educational Commitment
items.text: Teach, explain, document, research, clarify, preserve — reduce confusion not inflame controversy

items.title: Organizational Structure
items.text: Permanent departments; additional departments may be created as institution grows

items.title: Leadership
items.text: Stewardship — protect research quality, public trust, develop leaders, maintain transparency

items.title: Volunteer Foundation
items.text: All-volunteer institution; volunteers central to all functions

items.title: Financial Principles
items.text: Arkansas citizen contributions preferred; support never determines conclusions

items.title: Coalition
items.text: Collaboration with evidence-based orgs; each retains identity

items.title: Mission Control
items.text: Executive operating system — monitors all institutional domains

items.title: Research Standards
items.text: Documented, verified, reviewed, sourced; subject to continual improvement

items.title: Community Leadership
items.text: Academy → Neighborhood Leaders → City → County → Regional Mentors

items.title: Public Trust
items.text: Greatest asset — transparency, evidence, respect, consistency, accessibility, accountability

items.title: Amendment Process
items.text: Evolve preserving mission, transparency, stewardship; MC documents revisions

values.principle: Show sources, explain methods, document decisions
Dignity; welcome honest questions and thoughtful dialogue
Knowledge exists to serve the people of Arkansas
Every generation leaves the institution stronger

long_term_vision: Fifty years from now, still serving Arkansas. New technology, leaders, research, questions — purpose unchanged: help Arkansans understand constitutional system through research, evidence, education, and community leadership.

integration.chain: Founding Constitution → Governance → Volunteer & Funding → Master Plan → Mission Control → All institutional systems

catalog_gaps: 15 articles specified — no per-article public pages
0/10 departments operational — department registry not built
0 constitutional amendments recorded — amendment log not implemented
Adherence monitoring not live — MC does not enforce constitution checks
Build #76 vs Governance (#49) — reconcile founding vs governance constitutions?
Build #76 vs Project Constitution (#2) — document supersession chain
Build #76 vs Volunteer & Funding (#75) — Articles VIII-IX align but not linked in UI
Department heads unassigned — organizational structure aspirational
No public-facing constitution page on front door
Amendment process specified — no workflow for proposing amendments
MC Office partial — other 9 departments at 0% operational
Founder principle not displayed on site header/footer

recommended_next_build.title: Implementation Translation Layer & Engineering Artifacts
recommended_next_build.note: Article pages, department registry, amendment log, adherence checklist, public constitution route, COMP-* specs, GitHub backlog.
